query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Current SPR Level (EIA WCSSTUS1, week ending June 19, 2026): 331,191 thousand barrels (~331.2 million barrels)** — the lowest recent level, confirmed across EIA, YCharts, and TradingEconomics.

**Trajectory over the past 3-6 months — two distinct phases:**

1. **Slow fill (Dec 2025 → mid-March 2026):** Gradual additions of +0.2 to +0.8M bbl/week, rising from ~411.9M bbl to a 2026 peak of ~415.4M bbl (held early-to-mid March).

2. **Accelerating release (late March → June 2026):** The reserve flipped to drawdown the week ending March 27. Draws escalated from small amounts to a peak of −7.1M bbl (week ending April 24, largest since Oct 2022), then sustained at **~8–10 million barrels/week** through May and June. Total peak-to-latest decline: **~84.3 million barrels**.

**Driver:** A coordinated IEA ~400M bbl global emergency release responding to Middle East conflict supply disruptions, with the US committing 172M bbl structured as an exchange. The SPR now sits at ~46% of its ~714M bbl capacity and is down ~17.7% year-over-year.

All findings have been cross-validated and submitted via the structured output with full weekly data points and sourcing.

key_facts: Latest EIA SPR level (WCSSTUS1, week ending June 19, 2026): 331,191 thousand barrels (~331.2 million barrels).
2026 peak was ~415,442 thousand barrels, held from early-to-mid March 2026; the SPR has since fallen ~84.3 million barrels.
From Dec 2025 to mid-March 2026 the SPR was in slow fill mode (small weekly additions of +0.2 to +0.8M bbl).
Release mode began the week ending March 27, 2026, and accelerated to ~8-10 million barrels per week through May-June 2026.
Largest single weekly draw was -7.1 million barrels in the week ending April 24, 2026 (largest since week ending Oct 7, 2022).
DOE released 17.5 million barrels between weeks ending March 20 and April 24, 2026, per EIA.
Releases are part of a coordinated IEA ~400-million-barrel global release responding to Middle East conflict supply disruptions; US share is 172 million barrels, structured as an exchange.
SPR authorized storage capacity is ~714 million barrels; current level is ~46% of capacity.
Year-over-year, the SPR is down ~17.7% from ~402.3 million barrels one year earlier.
All figures are EIA weekly reported values in thousand barrels; data vintage is week ending June 19, 2026.
